rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths
Use only visible identifiers and declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid unresolved reference errors
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use valid AADL scope and type declarations without introducing undefined references
Generated AGREE expressions should preserve type consistency and avoid package-qualified identifiers not in model dependencies
Component type annexes should contain only visible feature-based contracts, not implementation-specific bindings
Repair diagnostics by aligning types and using only existing visible identifiers from the component scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax for assume, guarantee, eq, and const declarations
Use `eq` for helper functions and `const` for named values, ensuring type consistency in expressions
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation paths in component type annexes
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL property names as AGREE variables
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
